title: Methods for using dna methylation as a biomarker for identification and treatment of metabolic syndrome
abstract: Provided herein are methods relating to the use of DNA methylation as a biomarker for metabolic syndrome; methods of diagnosing metabolic syndrome in a subject; methods of selecting a subject for treatment to reduce one or more signs or symptoms of metabolic syndrome; and methods of treating a subject having metabolic syndrome.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of diagnosing metabolic syndrome in a subject, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from the subject; b) detecting the methylation status of one or more loci selected from the group consisting of L1NC01317IL1NC01320, MSH2/MSH6, AFTPH/SLC1A4 , AFAP1, BOD1/CPEB4 , BPHL, RXRA , CAMK2G , HPS1, FRMD8/SCYL1, PRICKLE1, TPCN1, RAP1GAP2 , RAMP2 , FASN, SBN02 , NRIP1/USP25 , and MNP, and c) diagnosing the subject with metabolic syndrome based upon the methylation status of the one or more loci. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the subject is diagnosed with metabolic syndrome if one or more loci selected from the group consisting of AFAP1, BOD1/CPEB4, BPHL , RXRA , CAMK2G , HPS1, TPCN1, RAMP 2 , LASN, SBN02 , NRIP1/USP25 , and A/L7 are methylated. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the subject is diagnosed with metabolic syndrome if one or more loci selected from the group consisting of LINC01317ILINC01320 , MSH2/MSH6, AFTPH/SLC1A4 , FRMD8/SCYL1, PRICKLE R and RAP1GAP2 are not methylated. 4. A method of diagnosing a subject as being likely to develop type II diabetes, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from the subject; b) detecting the methylation status of one or more loci set forth in Table 4; and c) diagnosing the subject as being likely to develop type II diabetes based upon the methylation status of the one or more loci. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the subject is diagnosed as being likely to develop type II diabetes if each of the loci set forth in Table 4 are methylated. 6. The method of claim 4 or claim 5, wherein the subject is prescribed one or more preventative therapies if one or more loci set forth in Table 4 are methylated. 7. A method of diagnosing metabolic syndrome in a subject, comprising: 

 a) obtaining a sample from the subject; b) detecting the methylation status of one or more loci selected from the group consisting of LINC01317 ILINC01320, MSH2/MSH6, AFTPH/SLC1A4 , AFAP1, BOD1/CPEB4 , BPHL, RXRA , CAMK2G , HPS1, FRMD8/SCYL1, PRICKLE P TPCN1, RAP1GAP2 , RAMP2 , /ÎLA, L7/LU/2, NRIP1/USP25 , and MNP, c) comparing the methylation status of the one or more loci to a reference methylation status; d) determining that the methylation status of the one or more loci is significantly different than the reference methylation status; and e) diagnosing the subject with metabolic syndrome. 8. A method of treating a subject having metabolic syndrome, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from the subject; b) detecting the methylation status of one or more loci selected from the group consisting of LINC01317 ILINC01320, MSH2/MSH6, AFTPH/SLC1A4, AFAP1, BOD1/CPEB4, BPHL, RXRA, CAMK2G, HPS1, FRMD8/SCYL1, PRICKLE1, TPCN1, RAP1GAP2, RAMP2, FASN , SBN02, NRIP1/USP25, and MNP, c) comparing the methylation status of the one or more loci to a reference methylation status; d) determining that the methylation status of the one or more loci is significantly different than the reference methylation status; and e) administering to the subject an effective amount of a therapeutic agent that reduces one or more signs or symptoms of metabolic syndrome. 9. A method of selecting a subject for treatment with a therapeutic agent that reduces one or more signs or symptoms of metabolic syndrome, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from the subject; b) detecting the methylation status of one or more loci selected from the group consisting of LINC01317 ILINC01320, MSH2/MSH6, AFTPH/SLC1A4, AFAP1, BOD1/CPEB4, BPHL, RXRA, CAMK2G, HPS1, FRMD8/SCYL1, PRICKLE1, TPCN1, RAP1GAP2, RAMP2, FASN SBN02, NRIP1/USP25, and MNP, 

 c) comparing the methylation status of the one or more loci to a reference methylation status; d) determining that the methylation status of the one or more loci is significantly different than the reference methylation status; and e) selecting the subject for treatment with the therapeutic agent. 10. A method of treating a subject having metabolic syndrome with a therapeutic agent, comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of the therapeutic agent wherein the methylation status of one or more loci selected from the group consisting of LINC01317/LINC01320, MSH2/MSH6, AFTPH/SLC1A4 , AFAP1, BOD1/CPEB4 , BPHL, RXRA , CAMK2G , HPS1, FRMD8/SC YL 1 , PRICKLE I TPCNl, RAP1GAP2 , RAMP2 , FASN, SBN02 , NRIP1/USP25 , and MN1 in a sample obtained from the subject was significantly different than a reference methylation status. 11. A method of treating a subject having metabolic syndrome with a therapeutic agent, comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of the therapeutic agent wherein the subject was selected for treatment based on a determination that the methylation status of one or more loci selected from the group consisting of LINC01317ILINC01320 , MSH2/MSH6, AFTPH/SLC1A4 , AFAP1, BOD1/CPEB4 , BPHL , RXRA , CAMK2G , HPS1, FRMD8/SCYL1, PRICKLE I, TPCNl , RAP1GAP2 , RAMP2 , FASN, SBN02 , NRIP1/USP25 , andMV7 in a sample obtained from the subject was significantly different than a reference methylation status. 12. A method of treating metabolic syndrome in a subject comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of a therapeutic agent wherein treatment is based upon the methylation status of one or more loci selected from the group consisting of LINC01317ILINC01320 , MSH2/MSH6, AFTPH/SLC1A4 , AFAP1, BOD1/CPEB4 , BPHL , RXRA , CAMK2G , JZ/Â¾7, FRMD8/SC YL 1 , PRICK! El TPCNl , RAP1GAP2 , RAMP2, FASN , SBN02 , NRLP1/USP25 , and A/L7 in a sample obtained from the subject being significantly different than a reference methylation status. 13. A method of treating metabolic syndrome in a subject provided that the methylation status of one or more loci selected from the group consisting of LINC01317ILINC01320 , MSH2/MSH6, AFTPH/SLC1A4 , AFAP7, BOD1/CPEB4 , BPHL, RXRA , CAMK2G , ///A7, FRMD8/SCYL1, PRICKLE I, TPCNl , RAP l GAP 2, RAMP2 , LASN, SBN02 , NRIP1/USP25 , 

 and A/A ' 7 in a sample obtained from the subject was found to be significantly different than a reference methylation status, the method comprising administering to the subject an effective amount of a therapeutic agent. 14. A method of diagnosing a subject as being likely to develop type II diabetes, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from the subject; b) detecting the methylation status of one or more loci set forth in Table 4; c) comparing the methylation status of the one or more loci to a reference methylation status; d) determining that the methylation status of the one or more loci is significantly different than the reference methylation status; and e) diagnosing the subject as being likely to develop type II diabetes. 15. A method of preventing the development of type II diabetes in a subject, comprising: a) obtaining a sample from the subject; b) detecting the methylation status of one or more loci set forth in Table 4; c) comparing the methylation status of the one or more loci to a reference methylation status; d) determining that the methylation status of the one or more loci is significantly different than the reference methylation status; and e) prescribing to the subject one or more preventative therapies that reduces the likelihood of developing type II diabetes. 16. The method of any one of claims 7-15, wherein the reference methylation status is the methylation status for a corresponding locus in a sample obtained from a healthy subject. 17. The method of any one of claims 7-15, wherein the reference methylation status is the average methylation status for a corresponding locus in a database of samples obtained from two or more healthy subjects. 18. The method of any one of claims 1-17, wherein the sample is selected from the group consisting of a urine sample, a blood sample, a plasma sample, a saliva sample, and a tissue biopsy. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the tissue biopsy is an adipose tissue biopsy. 20. The method of any one of claims 1-19, wherein the subject is a human. 21. The method of any one of claims 1-20, wherein the methylation status is detected by reduced representation bisulfite sequencing (RRBS). 22. The method of any one of claims 1-21, wherein detecting methylation status of the one or more loci comprises detecting the methylation status of a region associated with the one or more loci. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the region associated with the one or more loci is an intergenic region upstream or downstream of the one or more loci. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the intergenic region is within about 450 kilobases (kb) upstream or downstream of the one or more loci. 25. The method of claim 23 or claim 24, wherein the intergenic region is from about 23 kb to about 430 kb upstream or downstream of the one or more loci. 26. The method of claim 22, wherein the region associated with the one or more loci is an intragenic region within the one or more loci. 27. The method of any one of claims 22-26, wherein the region comprises at least 2 CpG dinucleotides. 28. The method of any one of claims 22-27, wherein the region comprises no more than about 10 cytosine residues. 29. The method of any one of claims 22-28, wherein the region comprises no more than about 3000 base pairs. 30. The method of any one of claims 22-29, wherein the region comprises about 3 base pairs to about 3000 base pairs. 31. The method of any one of claims 22-30, wherein the region associated with the LINC01317ILINC01320 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 34545726 to about base pair 34545903 of human chromosome 2. 32. The method of any one of claims 22-31, wherein the region associated with the MSH2/MSH6 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 47979865 to about base pair 47980000 of human chromosome 2. 33. The method of any one of claims 22-32, wherein the region associated with the AFTPH/SLC1A4 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 65064793 to about base pair 65064867 of human chromosome 2. 34. The method of any one of claims 22-33, wherein the region associated with the AFAP1 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 7774215 to about base pair 7774379 of human chromosome 4. 35. The method of any one of claims 22-34, wherein the region associated with the BOD1/CPEB4 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 173132627 to about base pair 173132883 of human chromosome 5. 36. The method of any one of claims 22-35, wherein the region associated with the BPHL locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 3132000 to about base pair 3132119 of human chromosome 6. 37. The method of any one of claims 22-36, wherein the region associated with the RXRA locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 137263485 to about base pair 137263573 of human chromosome 9. 38. The method of any one of claims 22-37, wherein the region associated with the CAMK2G locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 75600604 to about base pair 75600822 of human chromosome 10. 39. The method of any one of claims 22-38, wherein the region associated with the HPS1 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 100183346 to about base pair 100183441 of human chromosome 10. 40. The method of any one of claims 22-39, wherein the region associated with the FRMD8/SCYL1 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 65248602 to about base pair 65248737 of human chromosome 11. 41. The method of any one of claims 22-40, wherein the region associated with the PRICKLE1 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 42883183 to about base pair 42883320 of human chromosome 12. 42. The method of any one of claims 22-41, wherein the region associated with the TPCN1 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 113734359 to about base pair 113734579 of human chromosome 12. 43. The method of any one of claims 22-42, wherein the region associated with the RAP1GAP2 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 2893657 to about base pair 2893829 of human chromosome 17. 44. The method of any one of claims 22-43, wherein the region associated with the RAMP2 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 40913548 to about base pair 40913600 of human chromosome 17. 45. The method of any one of claims 22-44, wherein the region associated with the FASN locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to a region selected from the group consisting of about base pair 80041514 to about base pair 80042067 of human chromosome 17, about base pair 80046324 to about base pair 80047195 of human chromosome 17, about base pair 80050198 to about base pair 80050365 of human chromosome 17, about base pair 80051500 to about base pair 80053080 of human chromosome 17, and any combinations thereof. 46. The method of any one of claims 22-45, wherein the region associated with the SBN02 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 1148096 to about base pair 1148232 of human chromosome 19. 47. The method of any one of claims 22-46, wherein the region associated with the NRIP1/USP25 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 16661536 to about base pair 1661714 of human chromosome 21. 48. The method of any one of claims 22-47, wherein the region associated with the MN1 locus comprises a nucleotide sequence corresponding to about base pair 28151964 to about base pair 28152032 of human chromosome 22. 49. The method of any one of claims 7-13 and 16-48, wherein the subject is diagnosed with and/or treated for metabolic syndrome when the methylation status of the FASN locus is greater than the reference methylation status. 50. The method of any one of claims 7-13 and 16-49, wherein the subject is diagnosed with and/or treated for metabolic syndrome when the methylation status of the BOD1/CPEB4 locus is greater than the reference methylation status. 51. The method of any one of claims 7-13 and 16-50, wherein the subject is diagnosed with and/or treated for metabolic syndrome when the methylation status of the AFTPH/SLC1A4 locus is less than the reference methylation status.
